what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment tied to the design-source channel, pointing to a specific image asset URI.
observations: YAML frontmatter metadata block with no body content
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target uses the URI scheme image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-07
standouts: Target references a 'recent-pitches' image asset (p-07) in the design-source channel
It is a minimal attachment ledger entry with a URI-style pointer
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Follows a fixed schema: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
Target URI uses a custom image:// scheme with channel and asset path
concerns: File contains no actual image data or local path, only a URI reference
No fields for uploader, message context, MIME type, dimensions, or alt text
The meaning of 'p-07' and its relationship to a channel message is not explained in the file
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that links the design-source channel to a recent-pitch image asset by URI.
questions: Which channel message or pitch does this attachment belong to?
What is the actual content of image p-07?
Who added this attachment and what triggered it?
Is p-07 a stable identifier or a transient 'recent' reference?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record an attachment reference, and the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: It appears designed to catalog image attachments so channel content can reference media assets by a stable, addressable URI.
novelty: Use of a custom image:// URI scheme to locate channel-specific image assets.
contribution: Provides a durable, addressable attachment record that links a channel to a specific image asset by URI and records when it was added.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-94c586ae in the design-source channel registers an images attachment targeting image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-07 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register an image attachment against a design-source channel pitch asset for retrieval and provenance tracking.
